title: Who Patches What?
description:
## Drawing the Responsibility Line

During a security audit, two questions come up about the **Shared Responsibility Model**:

1. Who is responsible for patching the **guest operating system** on a company-managed **EC2** instance running Linux?
2. Who is responsible for patching the **underlying operating system and database engine host** of a managed **Amazon RDS** instance?

Which statement correctly assigns responsibility for both?

options.text: 1) The **customer** patches the EC2 guest OS; 2) **AWS** patches the underlying OS and engine of RDS (the customer still manages data, access, and engine-level configuration).
1) **AWS** patches the EC2 guest OS automatically; 2) the **customer** must SSH into the RDS host to patch its operating system.
AWS is responsible for everything in both cases because both run on AWS hardware ("security of the cloud" covers all patching).
The customer is responsible for everything in both cases, including the physical hosts and hypervisor underneath RDS.

explanation: The model splits responsibility at the service boundary. AWS is responsible for **security OF the cloud** — the physical facilities, hardware, hypervisor, and the managed-service infrastructure. The customer is responsible for **security IN the cloud** — their data, IAM configuration, network/firewall rules, and, crucially, the **guest OS on EC2** (EC2 is IaaS, so OS patching is yours). With **RDS** (a managed PaaS), AWS operates the host OS and database engine binaries and applies engine patches, so **AWS** handles that OS patching; the customer still owns the data, accounts, security groups, parameter groups, and choosing/scheduling engine version upgrades. Option B inverts both: you never SSH into an RDS host, and AWS does not patch your EC2 guest OS. Options C and D incorrectly collapse the shared line entirely to one party.

hints.title: IaaS vs. managed service
hints.body: On EC2 (IaaS) you own the OS upward. On RDS (managed) AWS runs the OS and engine for you; you stay responsible for data and access.

hints.title: The phrasing to remember
hints.body: AWS = security OF the cloud (hardware, hypervisor, managed-service infra). Customer = security IN the cloud (data, IAM, EC2 OS patching).
